name: Webhook Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Webhook Workflow. This workflow integrates 18 different services: webhook, stickyNote, httpRequest, filter, markdown. It contains 42 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## What does it do?
Objective:

Streamline the onboarding process for new customers, ensuring they receive all necessary resources and support.
Trigger: Set a webhook trigger or a CRM trigger (like HubSpot or Salesforce) for when a new customer is added.

Send Welcome Email: Use the Gmail or SMTP node to send a personalized welcome email to the customer.

Schedule a Welcome Call: Use the Calendar node (Google Calendar) to automatically create a calendar event for a welcome call.

Assign a CSM: Use the CRM node (like HubSpot) to assign the new customer to a dedicated CSM.

nodes.name: Calendar Agent

options.systemMessage:
=# Overview
You are a calendar assistant. Your responsibilities include creating, getting, and deleting events in the user's calendar.
If no date is proposed, find the next available slot using "Get Events" and create an event using "Create Event with Attendee"

**Calendar Management Tools**  
   - Use "Create Event with Attendee" when an event includes a participant.     
   - Use "Get Events" to fetch calendar schedules when requested.
   - Use "Delete Event" to delete an event. You must use "Get Events" first to get the ID of the event to delete.
   - Use "Update Event" to update an event. You must use "Get Events" first to get the ID of the event to update.

## Final Notes
Here is the current date/time: {{ $now }}
If a duration for an event isn't specified, assume it will be one hour.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## How to Set a Webhook in n8n and HubSpot

## 1. Set Up a Webhook in n8n

### Step 1: Create a New Workflow
- Go to your **n8n dashboard**.
- Click on **"New Workflow."**

### Step 2: Add the Webhook Node
- Click on the **“+”** icon to add a new node.
- Search for **“Webhook”** and select it.
- Set the **Webhook Method** (usually POST).
- Define the **Webhook URL path**, for example, `/hubspot-webhook`.
- Set the **"Response Mode"** (e.g., "On Received").
- Save the workflow.

### Step 3: Set Webhook URL
- Copy the **Webhook URL** generated by n8n. It should look something like:{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

- Ensure the workflow is **active**.

---

## 2. Set Up a Webhook in HubSpot

### Step 1: Log in to HubSpot
- Go to **HubSpot Developer Account** (required for webhook setup).
- Navigate to **"Settings" > "Integrations" > "Webhooks."**

### Step 2: Create a New Webhook Subscription
- Click **“Create Webhook”** or **“Add Webhook”** if this is your first one.
- Select the **events** you want to track (e.g., contact creation, form submission).
- Set the **Webhook URL** as the n8n Webhook URL you copied earlier.
- Choose **“POST”** as the request method.
- Set the **Authentication** if needed (you can set a secret or use OAuth).

### Step 3: Test the Webhook
- Use the **“Test Webhook”** feature in HubSpot to send a test request.
- Switch to n8n and ensure the webhook is triggering properly.

---

## 3. Process the Data in n8n
- After the webhook is triggered in n8n, you will see the data sent by HubSpot.
- You can now add additional nodes to **process the data** (e.g., save to database, send email, perform actions in another app).

---

## 4. Make the Workflow Active
- Once you are done configuring, make sure the workflow is **set to “Active.”**
- This will allow it to receive live data from HubSpot.

options.systemMessage:
=# Overview
You are a professional Customer Success Manager.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Calendar  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
hubspot  O Auth2  A P I details
hubspot Developer  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "When Executed by Another Workflow".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "HubSpot Trigger".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calendar Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Event with Attendee" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Event" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Events" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete Event" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Event" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model2" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "calendarAgent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get list of owners" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out owners" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get current owner" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If a contact is created" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all info about the contact" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Write a personalized message" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send the message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Set owner to contact" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Transforms markdown to HTML" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Try Again".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Success".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Enter your company data here".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
